title: Python lists
text: Python lists are ordered mutable collections. You can append, remove, and slice elements easily.
author: alice

title: Python dictionaries
text: Dictionaries store key-value pairs. Keys must be hashable. Lookup is O(1) on average.
author: alice

title: Binary search
text: Binary search finds an element in a sorted array in O(log n) time by repeatedly halving the search space.
author: bob

title: Hash tables
text: A hash table uses a hash function to map keys to buckets. Collisions are resolved by chaining or open addressing.
author: bob

title: SQL joins
text: SQL joins combine rows from two tables. INNER JOIN returns matching rows, LEFT JOIN includes all rows from the left table.
author: carol

title: Database indexing
text: Indexes speed up query execution by allowing the database engine to find rows without scanning the full table.
author: carol

title: Docker containers
text: Docker packages applications and their dependencies into containers, ensuring consistent environments across machines.
author: dave

title: Docker networking
text: Docker networks allow containers to communicate. Bridge mode is the default; host mode shares the host network stack.
author: dave

title: Gradient descent
text: Gradient descent minimizes a loss function by iteratively moving in the direction of the negative gradient.
author: eve

title: Neural networks
text: Neural networks are composed of layers of neurons. Each neuron applies a weighted sum and an activation function.
author: eve

title: Vector databases
text: Vector databases store and index high-dimensional embeddings, enabling fast approximate nearest-neighbor search.
author: frank

title: Retrieval-augmented generation
text: RAG systems retrieve relevant documents from a knowledge base and provide them as context to a language model.
author: frank
